title: [main.c:109] Missing LoRa deinit on cleanup path
description:
What: init_standalone_lora() calls lichen_lora_l2_stop() and lichen_lora_l2_deinit() on the eui64==NULL error path, but if lichen_lora_l2_start() fails (line 100-104), it only returns the error without cleanup.

Why it matters: After start() fails:
1. lichen_lora_l2_init() has succeeded (EUI-64 generated, device configured)
2. State is STOPPED (init transitions UNINIT->STOPPED)
3. Subsequent retry of init_standalone_lora() would succeed at init() (idempotent) but might have stale state

This is minor because the error path leads to main() entering an infinite error-blink loop (lines 196-215), so there's no retry. But the asymmetric cleanup is a code smell.

Evidence:
```c
ret = lichen_lora_l2_start();
if (ret < 0) {
    LOG_ERR("main: LoRa L2 start failed (%d)", ret);
    return ret;  // No cleanup, unlike cleanup: path at line 127
}
```
close_reason: Not a bug. The cleanup path at lines 130-133 already calls stop() and deinit() when needed (after start() succeeded). Early returns at lines 100-101 and 106-107 occur when state is STOPPED (init succeeded but start failed/wasn't reached) - no active resources to clean up. The device enters an infinite error loop anyway, and power cycle resets everything. Documented: device stays initialized in STOPPED state, which is intentional and leak-free.
